title: dacryodes olivifera
intro: Dacryodes olivifera, also known as the African pear or iroko, is a tropical evergreen tree native to West Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Dacryodes olivifera include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and other digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
